segments.caption: In a sunlit park, the Older man, wearing a white polo shirt with a bandaged hand and a pink wristband, sits on a green wooden bench and speaks earnestly to the camera, sharing his thoughts on life, freedom, and a tragic event from 1989. He gestures with his hands as he recounts how a young woman and her two-year-old child died in a car accident while driving from Chisinau, emphasizing the suddenness of their fate. Beside him, the Man in gray t-shirt, wearing a light gray t-shirt with small patterns and striped shorts, listens intently, occasionally crossing his arms or shifting his posture, his expression thoughtful and engaged. The background features a paved path lined with trees, dappled sunlight, and occasional passersby, including a child running and a person on a scooter, contributing to a calm yet reflective atmosphere. Subtitles appear on screen, capturing the Older man’s words: 'They are rude and swear a lot,' 'I don’t want to waste my time on them,' 'I am free and have the people I meet,' 'I have a life full of freedom,' 'In 1989,' 'They were driving from Chisinau in a car,' 'They decided to overtake a vehicle,' 'and there was a truck coming from the opposite direction,' 'They hit that truck at a very high speed,' 'They died immediately,' 'Yes, it seems it was their destiny,' 'Was it her fault?,' 'She was 19 and her child was 2.' The scene maintains a steady, intimate focus on the two men, with the Older man’s emotional narrative contrasting with the serene park setting.
